title: vernonia ferruginea var. polycephala
intro: Vernonia ferruginea var. polycephala, also known as the Ironweed or Red Ironweed, is a perennial plant species that belongs to the daisy family Asteraceae. It is traditionally used in folk medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial activities. The plant is believed to offer various health benefits, such as reducing fever, relieving pain, and treating digestive issues, as well as supporting the immune system. Vernonia ferruginea var. polycephala can be prepared as teas, infusions, or tinctures, and its leaves and roots are often used in traditional herbal remedies.
